title: Interactive Chart Testing with Button Logic and Statistical Levels
subtitle: Volume-Weighted Statistics, Asymmetric Z-Score Thresholds, KDE Mode Detection
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: LOW-MEDIUM EXTRACT — extends Part 41 with volume-weighted statistics and asymmetric thresholds (long z >= 2.0, short z <= -0.8). Interactive UI irrelevant for Python. KDE bandwidth=1.0 for mode estimation.

extensions_vs_part_41.volume_weighted_stats: UseWeightedByVol=true: weight each TP by its volume. VWAP-style distribution.
extensions_vs_part_41.asymmetric_thresholds: Long entry: z >= +2.0; Short entry: z <= -0.8 (asymmetric — easier to go short). Suggests bearish bias in the tested instrument.
extensions_vs_part_41.kde_mode: KDEGridPoints=100, BandwidthFactor=1.0 (Silverman's rule). Mode = peak of density estimate.

formulas.vwap_tp: vwap = sum(tp[i] * vol[i]) / sum(vol[i]) — volume-weighted mean
formulas.kde_bandwidth: h = 1.06 * std(data) * n^(-0.2) * BandwidthFactor — Silverman's rule
formulas.kde_mode: Find peak of gaussian KDE evaluated over KDEGridPoints grid

keep: Asymmetric threshold concept (instrument may have directional bias)
KDE for mode with Silverman bandwidth
discard: Asymmetric thresholds (we'd calibrate per instrument from DNA data)
improve: Use volume-weighted TP for all statistical computations (Part 41 + 42 combined)
Calibrate z-score thresholds per instrument using DNA smoothness_index
